class.name: AudioStreamOggVorbis
class.brief_description: Stores audio data in Ogg Vorbis format as an [AudioStream] resource.
class.description: [AudioStreamOggVorbis] stores audio samples from loaded Ogg Vorbis files. Ogg Vorbis files can be loaded with [method load_from_file]. See [ResourceImporterOggVorbis] for information on how Ogg Vorbis files are imported as [AudioStream] resources. To play the stored audio, use an [AudioStreamPlayer] (for non-positional audio) or [AudioStreamPlayer2D]/[AudioStreamPlayer3D] (for positional audio). This stream is ideal to hold music, since this format provides higher quality than [AudioStreamMP3] at a given bit rate, and is generally small in size. It has a [i]higher[/i] CPU cost to decode compared to [AudioStreamWAV], so it is recommended to use WAV if you need many playbacks.

method.name: load_from_buffer
method.description: Creates a new [AudioStreamOggVorbis] instance from the given buffer. The buffer must contain Ogg Vorbis data.

method.name: load_from_file
method.description: Creates a new [AudioStreamOggVorbis] instance from the given file path. The file must be in Ogg Vorbis format. [b]Note:[/b] This method should only be used to load [i]external[/i] files at runtime. To load files that were previously imported into Godot as resources, use a method like [method @GDScript.load].

members.member: The number of beats within a single bar of the audio.

The length of the audio, in beats. The actual duration of the audio file might be longer than what is indicated by this property. It defines the end of the audio for looping, [AudioStreamPlaylist], and [AudioStreamInteractive].
The tempo of the audio, measured in beats per minute (BPM).

If [code]true[/code], the stream will play again from the specified [member loop_offset] once it reaches the end of the stream, or once it reaches the end of the last beat according to the number specified in [member beat_count]. Useful for ambient sounds and background music.

Playback position in seconds at which the stream starts after being looped. This value should range from [code]0.0[/code] to the length of the stream in seconds, or to the end of the beat set in [member beat_count] in seconds, if the stream loops at a set beat.
Contains the raw Ogg data for this stream.

Contains user-defined tags if found in the Ogg Vorbis data. Commonly used tags include [code]title[/code], [code]artist[/code], [code]album[/code], [code]tracknumber[/code], and [code]date[/code] ([code]date[/code] does not have a standard date format). [b]Note:[/b] No tag is [i]guaranteed[/i] to be present in every file, so make sure to account for the keys not always existing.
